name: Summer Streets: Manhattan
description:
Park Avenue and connecting streets from Brooklyn Bridge to Broadway and Dyckman Street will be open for people to play, walk, and bike from 7am to 3pm. 
 Our city's usual traffic noise and honking horns will be replaced with open streets teeming with walkers, bikers, runners, and performers. Discover a wealth of free cultural programs, performances, fitness classes, interactive art, giveaways, and much more as you experience the city in this whole new way. 
 Route 
 
 Lafayette Street and Park Avenue between Brooklyn Bridge and East 110 Street 
 East 110 Street between Park Avenue and Broadway 
 Adam Clayton Powell Jr Boulevard between West 110 and West 113 Street 
 Broadway between 110 Street and Dyckman Street 
 
 This year's event occurs on July 25 in Queens and Staten Island, August 1, 8, and 15 in Manhattan, and August 22 in Brooklyn and the Bronx. 
 Visit nyc.gov/summerstreets for location details and programming updates. 
 Summers Streets is a rain or shine event.
location_name: Brooklyn Bridge to Broadway and Dyckman Street
address: Brooklyn Bridge to Broadway and Dyckman Street
secondary_categories: Free,Street and Neighborhood,Kids and Family,Featured,General Events

name: Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all. Experience a fun and light-hearted, mid-intensity flow that will move you seamlessly from Sun Salutation to a final peak pose. We focus on inversions and breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced. Mats are recommended. Class meets on the lawn southeast of the tennis courts. Adjacent to the outdoor fitness gym.
address: Tennis Courts in Pelham Bay Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Baisley Pond Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Baisley Pond Park is located near JFK Airport in Springfield Gardens, Queens on the border of NYC and Nassau County. The park features tennis, handball, and basketball courts; peaceful, shady alcoves for picnicking; and plenty of plant and animal wildlife.
address: Baisley Pond Park House in Baisley Pond Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Canarsie Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Canarsie Park serves the neighborhoods of Canarsie and nearby Flatlands, Mill Basin, Marine Park, and Bergen Beach. Home to baseball fields and cricket pitches, it's bordered by the Belt Parkway and Paerdegat Basin.
address: Canarsie Park House in Canarsie Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Crocheron Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Widely known for its history with the Crocheron Family and summer house, Crocheron Park is home to running paths, baseball fields, and a 4-court tennis center.
address: Entrance to John Golden Park in John Golden Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Highland Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Located in the Cypress Hills section of Brooklyn, Highland Park is close to East New York and Bushwick in Brooklyn and the Woodhaven, Ridgewood, and Glendale neighborhoods in Queens. It's home to over 150 species of birds and to the Ridgewood Reservoir.
address: Elton Street and Jamaica Avenue in Highland Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Inwood Hill Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Evidence of Inwood Hill Park's formation exists as caves, valleys, and ridges left as the result of shifting glaciers. Evidence of its uninhabited state afterward remains as its forest and salt marsh (the last natural one in Manhattan), and evidence of its use by Native Americans continues to be discovered. Most of the park was largely untouched by wars and development. The park features a hiking trail and the Hudson River Bike Trail, and is one of the best places in NYC to spot a bald eagle. It also includes athletic fields, playgrounds, dog runs, and a barbecue area. The course is three loops on hilly trails and walkways through woods and along a salt marsh.
location_name: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance
address: Seaman Avenue and Isham Street Entrance in Inwood Hill Park

name: NYRR Open Run: Shore Road Park
description: NYRR Open Run Brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Shore Road Park, located in Bay Ridge, Brooklyn, is one of our longer Open Run courses. The park serves residents of Bay Ridge, Sunset Park, Dyker Heights, Fort Hamilton, and other surrounding neighborhoods. The course is three loops alongside Shore Road with stunning views of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge.
address: Lawn Near 79th Street Entrance in Shore Park and Parkway

name: NYRR Open Run: Soundview Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. Called the \"Gateway to the Bronx River,\" 205-acre Soundview Park is located where the Bronx River opens into the East River. This urban green space is filled with grassy baseball and soccer fields, a cricket pitch, basketball courts, a running track, walking\/biking paths, picnic fields, and more! With its extensive views of the water, Soundview Park celebrates the beauty of both the Bronx and East Rivers. The back part of our Open Run course has beautiful views of the Bronx River waterfront for participants to enjoy!
location_name: Entrance - Lafayette Avenue and Morrison Avenue
address: Entrance - Lafayette Avenue and Morrison Avenue in Soundview Park

name: NYRR Open Run: St. Marys Park
description: NYRR Open Run brings free, 5K weekly community-led runs, to neighborhood parks across the greater New York City area. The program is free and open to runners and walkers of all ages, abilities, and experience levels. St. Mary's Park offers a wonderful respite from the hustle and bustle of urban life. Located in the Mott Haven neighborhood in the South Bronx, the park features rolling hills, expanses of green grass, and grand old trees. The site was once part of the estate of Jonas Bronck (1600-43), for whom the Bronx is named. It is one of the largest parks in the area with a pool, playground, basketball courts, an indoor recreation center, picnic seating areas, and more. In the winter, the hills are perfect for sledding. St. Mary's Park received $30 million from NYC's Anchor Parks initiative, which will renovate the park's ballfields, pathways, and amphitheater.
address: St. Mary's Annual 5K Start

name: Kids In Motion: Addabbo Playground
description: The Kids in Motion program engages children in active, outdoors play. A NYC Parks staff member leads four to seven hours of free activities in NYC's playgrounds, including organized sports, games, fitness demos, board games, and more! Kids in Motion is a free, drop-in program without registration. Children can head to the playground during the listed hours of operation to participate. Occasionally a Kids in Motion program may be cancelled due to harsh weather or other conditions. Please note: We recommend that guardians accompany children participating in this program. Kids in Motion is not a childcare program and NYC parks is not responsible for the well-being of attendees.
address: Addabbo Playground in Tudor Park

name: Moves with Limón Dance at Bryant Park
description: Move to live accompaniment and experience the joy of dance with dancers from the Limón. Open to all levels and ages - no experience required! Bring friends, family, water on hot days, and enjoy dancing on the grass. Class meets on the northeast corner of the lawn.
address: Bryant Park Lawn in Bryant Park

name: NYC Public Beaches
description:
Parks maintains 14 miles of beaches, which open Memorial Day weekend. During beach season, lifeguards are on duty daily, from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. Swimming is prohibited when lifeguards are not on duty and in closed sections. Closed sections are marked with signs and/or red flags. 
 
 Sign up for Notify NYC and opt-in to "beach notifications" to receive real-time opening and closing notifications. 
 Photo credit: Benny Polatseck/Mayoral Photography Office
address: NYC Public Beaches Citywide

name: Rise&Restore Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full body workout. Whether you are a beginner or looking for a challenge; this class for all fitness levels and modifications are always provided. Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups, get your heart pumping, and connect with people in your community!
location_name: Dance Room in Highbridge Recreation Center
address: Dance Room in Highbridge Recreation Center

name: Strong For Life Circuit Training
description: A welcoming exercise class focused on helping participants feel stronger, more mobile, and more energized in daily life. Class includes low-impact strength and endurance exercises, mobility training, functional fitness and balance work. Modifications will be provided for all levels of fitness.
address: BPL- Macon Library

name: Summer Sports Experience: St. Mary's Park \"Game of Zones Girls Basketball Clinic\"
description: If you love basketball and want to improve your skills, join game of zones at St. Mary's Park ! Whether you are a beginner or experienced player who wants to come and improve their skills and play, do it with us! Saturdays | 10:00 a.m. - 11:00 a.m. | 8 - 11 yrs. Saturdays | 11:30 a.m. - 2:00 p.m. | 12 - 14 yrs.
address: Basketball Courts in St. Mary's Park

name: Yoga in the Park
description: Stretch, breathe, and find your inner peace at Randall's Island Park's free outdoor yoga series. Guided by a beloved, local yoga instructor, participants will flow through a calming and grounding sequence against the backdrop of the uptown skyline. All levels and abilities are welcome. Bring a friend, family member or two, or your yoga-loving pet! No registration required-just show up ready to move! We'll have water and a limited number of mats available to borrow, so please bring your own if possible or show up early to claim one. Our free yoga classes run weekly on Tuesdays from 6:30 - 7:30 pm from May 5 - September 1, and Saturdays from 10:00 - 11:00 am, July 11 - August 29. On Saturdays, stay after and join our dance class! Please check Instagram for the most updated schedule. Location: East River Picnic ...
address: East River Picnic Area in Randall's Island Park

name: Zumba
description: Zumba is a fusion of Latin, International and popular music dance themes creating a dynamic, exciting, effective fitness system! The routines feature aerobic, fitness interval training with a combination of fast and slow rhythms that tone and sculpt the body with easy to follow dance steps.
location_name: Gymnasium in St. John’s Recreation Center
address: Gymnasium in St. John’s Recreation Center

name: Bodyweight Burn Circuit Training
description: A bodyweight circuit class combining cardio, strength, and core-focused movements for a dynamic full-body workout! This class welcomes all fitness levels.
address: Brooklyn Public Library - Brooklyn Heights in Cadman Plaza Park

name: Shoreline Cleanup
description: Inwood Canoe Club and Friends of Inwood Hill Park are recruiting volunteers to clean up our beloved Hudson River shoreline. We'll be collecting trash to keep our waterfront community clean. Trash bags, work gloves, latex gloves, and grabbers will be provided. Feel free to bring your own work gloves and water bottle. Be sure to complete your 2026 season waiver before you arrive at the boathouse. \n Inwood Canoe Club en asociación con Friends of Inwood Hill Park están reclutando voluntarios para limpiar nuestra querida orilla del río Hudson. Recogeremos basura para limpiar nuestra comunidad litoral. Proporcionaremos bolsas de basura, guantes de trabajo, junto con algunos guantes de látex y agarradores. Puedes traer tus propios guantes de trabajo y una botella de agua. No olvides completar tu formulario Smart Waiver antes de venir a Inwood Canoe Club.
location_name: Inwood Canoe Club in Inwood Hill Park
address: 360 Dyckman Street

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. *Participants must bring a lock and lock up all personal belongings.
location_name: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center
address: Rock Climbing Wall Room in Gertrude Ederle Recreation Center

name: Basic Canoeing
description: Few experiences compare with being on the open water in New York City. The rhythm of the waves, the sun on your face, and the exhilaration of slicing through the water all add up to an experience you'll never forget. Our trained Urban Park Rangers will lead you on canoe adventures that range from the gentle waters of protected lakes to the challenging open waters of rivers and bays. All canoe programs are for ages 8 years and older. Meet at Cloverdale Boulevard entrance near 46th Avenue.
address: Oakland Lake in Alley Pond Park

name: Best Special Kids: Horticulture and Art Workshop with Gisselle Ramirez
description:
Join Giselle of Best Special Kids for this 1.5-2hr workshop that engages children with disabilities and their families in a sensory-friendly horticulture and art experience. Participants will decorate nursery pots and plant herbs or small vegetables, combining creativity with hands-on learning. 
 The workshop is designed to be inclusive and accessible, with adapted materials, visual supports, and flexible participation. Caregivers are encouraged to participate, strengthening family engagement and reinforcing learning at home. 
 Through this workshop, participants will build fine motor and creative skills, learn basic gardening practices, and develop confidence in a supportive environment. The session also introduces families to ongoing community-based, nature-centered programming. 
 Our goal is to create an inclusive space where children can connect with nature, express themselves, and build meaningful skills while fostering a sense of belonging.
address: Shoelace Park - Bronx Boulevard and 228th Street

name: Cardio Punch
description: Cardio Punch is a total body, non-contact workout fit for all levels. Punch, hook, and kick to motivating, high-energy music that progress into heart-pumping combinations.
address: Boardwalk at Beach 60th Street in Rockaway Beach and Boardwalk

name: Community Paddle at Starlight Park
description: Join the Bronx River Alliance for a free community paddle at Starlight Park! Come see your neighborhood from the river's perspective! This event is first come, first served with no pre-registration available. \"Waves\" of paddlers will enjoy the river for about 30-40 minutes, after signing waivers and receiving a brief paddling safety orientation. A total of 4 waves of participants will be able to go on-water every hour of the event (11am, 12pm, 1pm, 2pm), with a maximum of about 15 participants per wave. Staff and volunteers will be on-water to ensure safety, but please keep in mind that this is not a guided tour of the river. Minors must be accompanied by an adult. Waivers for minors will need to be signed by an adult guardian.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: Dance Fitness
description: Join Shape Up NYC for a free dance fitness class every Saturday at West Harlem Piers Park! The instructor for this class will change week to week. This summer the following instructors will be featured: Donnette \"Sonnia\" McFarlane, Claudia Gomez, Taneka Teel, Beverly Brown, Carolina Morales, Yhajira Muniz, Masayo Kado, Nathalie Roussel, Regina Nicosia, Erica Zhang, and Alejandra Villadiego. This class is appropriate for all fitness levels.
address: 125th Street and Marginal Street in West Harlem Piers

name: Dance in the Park: Merengue
description: Bring your energy and get ready to move at Merengue in the Park! Set against the backdrop of the waterfront and summer breeze, this lively class focuses on rhythm, connection, and fun. Participants will learn easy-to-follow steps and enjoy the fast-paced spirit of Merengue with an enthusiastic instructor leading the way. Perfect for all skill levels, this is a great opportunity to try something new or refine your moves. Come early for our 10 AM yoga class to warm up beforehand! Location: East River Picnic Area For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
address: East River Picnic Area in Randall's Island Park

name: Fordham Road BID Sidewalk Sale
description: Sidewalk Sale Learn about related weekend street closures Learn about related weekly street closures Visit the NYC Street Closures Map
location_name: EAST FORDHAM ROAD between JEROME AVENUE and WASHINGTON AVENUE  Bronx
address: EAST FORDHAM ROAD between JEROME AVENUE and WASHINGTON AVENUE  Bronx

name: Incense and Smoke Wand Workshop with Lashette Williams
description: Learn from Lashette how to make loose incense and smoke wands with local plants grown at the Bronx River Foodway. Participants will be introduced to the history and uses of plants and make their own incense blends. Note: This is one of this year's Community Grant workshops.
address: Concrete Plant Park

name: Indigenous Art Workshop with NAIC-NY
description: Join the the North American Indigenous Center of New York and the Bronx River Alliance for an Indigenous Art Workshop at Starlight Park! This family and kid-friendly event will offer participants an opportunity to learn traditional indigenous crafts, and gain exposure to contemporary indigenous cultures. This activity is first come, first served. This project is made possible with funds from the Statewide Community Regrants Program, a regrant program of the New York State Council on the Arts with support of the Office of the Governor and the New York State Legislature and administered by the Bronx Council on the Arts.
address: Bronx River House in Starlight Park

name: Nature's Workshop: Sandcastles and Beachcombing
description: On National Sandcastle Day, explore how sand and water work together to make strong sandcastles and try fun experiments on the beach. We'll also go beachcombing along the shoreline to look for shells, stones, and other natural treasures while learning how tides and waves shape what we find. It's a day of sandy science, shoreline discovery and hands\u2011on learning.
location_name: Entrance - Cornelia Avenue and Chester Avenue
address: Entrance - Cornelia Avenue and Chester Avenue in Wolfe's Pond Park

name: Ongoing Museum Exhibit: New York's Brick & Terra Cotta Industries
description: NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts and masonry bricks that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States, and to learn about the area's rich terracotta and brick making tradition, including the famed former Atlantic Terra Cotta Company.
address: H.H. Biddle House in Conference House Park

name: Ongoing Museum Exhibit: Out of the Box Biodiversity: Student Art
description: Out Of The Box Biodiversity Upcycled Cardboard Sculpture, Raku Fired Clay, Watercolor, Pen & Ink Donna Napoli-Steele Presents Youth Art Featuring Pieces Completed by her Students Out Of The Box Biodiversity Upcycled Cardboard Sculpture, Raku Fired Clay, Watercolor, Pen & Ink
address: Rutan-Becket House in Conference House Park

name: Ongoing Museum Exhibit: Still Waters: Reflections of a Maritime Community
description: NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to an exhibit celebrating the South Shore of Staten Island's maritime history and heritage, featuring the A.C. Brown & Sons Shipyard of Tottenville and spotlighting our recreational waterfront and more. Note location: Rutan-Becket House, 6 Shore Rd. Open by chance or by appointment.
address: Rutan-Becket House in Conference House Park

name: Ongoing Outdoor Museum Exhibit: Alice's Terracotta Garden
description: Terra cotta sculpture garden featuring antique architectural ornaments from Tottenville's Atlantic Terra Cotta Company (west side lawn of Biddle House). NYC Parks and the Tottenville Historical Society invite you to visit a unique collection of terracotta artifacts that chronicle turn-of-the-twentieth-century architectural styles common in New York City and across the United States.
address: H.H. Biddle House in Conference House Park

name: Phoenix Community Garden Farm Stand
description: Phoenix Community Garden's Farm Stand is open for the season, every Saturday rain or shine until mid-November. Each bag contains local farm fresh fruit, salad greens, the best seasonal vegetables, and aromatics and herbs for flavor. There is sliding scale pricing. Cards, tap to pay and EBT are accepted at the register. Learn more and pre-order on the garden's website here . Pick up at Phoenix Community Garden, 24 Somers Street, between 11:00 a.m. and 3:00 p.m.
address: Phoenix Community Garden

name: Salt Marsh Ecology Hike
description: Join the Urban Park Rangers for an interactive walk exploring how the landscape and ecology of Marine Park has been shaped by landfill. Learn about the efforts made to mitigate climate change, conserve natural resources and ecosystem services.
address: Salt Marsh Nature Center in Marine Park

name: Total Body Reset
description: This fun low-impact full-body workout includes cardio, strength and balance exercises to improve your overall fitness. Class will meet on the Greene Avenue lawn area.
address: Herbert Von King Park

name: Turtle Quest: Explore, Observe, Protect with Lizbeth Miron
description: Join Lizbeth for an exploration of the freshwater turtles that reside in the Bronx River in Shoelace Park, while learning about local ecology and conservation. We will learn to identify native and non-native turtle species and use iNaturalist, a community science app, to document wildlife observations. This workshop also includes a park clean-up to help protect turtle habitats and support the health of the Bronx River ecosystem. The goals of the workshop are to increase awareness of urban wildlife, encourage community involvement in conservation, and empower participants to contribute to environmental research and stewardship in their local parks.
address: Shoelace Park - Bronx Boulevard and 228th Street

name: Bodyweight Circuit Training
description: This circuit training class combines bodyweight resistance training and conditioning to provide you with a dynamic full-body workout. Whether you are a beginner who wants to learn proper form or know your way around the gym and looking for a challenge- this class is for you! Come engage in exercises that target all muscle groups and will get your heart pumping.
location_name: Dance Room in Chelsea Recreation Center
address: Dance Room in Chelsea Recreation Center

name: Chair Yoga
description: Connect with yourself in Chair Yoga, a class to be enjoyed by all- but designed for those who prefer to be seated. Experience a fun and light-hearted class that provides a seated flow. The goal is to raise your heart rate, challenge all muscle groups and improve balance while remaining seated with minimal impact on joints. Enjoy breath work to ensure you leave class feeling challenged and balanced!
location_name: Ballroom in Fort Hamilton Senior Recreation Center
address: Ballroom in Fort Hamilton Senior Recreation Center

name: Dance Fitness
description: Dance Fitness is a creative workout that fuses dance and aerobics. The goal is to improve cardio fitness while having fun and getting a full body workout! This class is taught to music with easy-to-follow steps that will surely get your heart pumping!
address: Brooklyn Public Library - Bay Ridge

name: One Book One Bronx book club
description: Come join the garden group at Bronx River Community Garden for a One Book One Bronx event! This is a weekly community space that sparks powerful conversations and celebrates diverse voices. Saturdays, May 23-August 8, registration required.
address: Bronx River Community Garden

name: Summer on the Hudson: Billion Oyster Project Presentation
description: Come see and learn about the Billion Oyster Project on the Baylander's Jungle Deck. It's the latest effort to restore our harbor!
address: On the Pier in West Harlem Piers

name: Summer Sports Experience: Basketball Seasonal Clinics at Faber Recreation Center
description: The Summer Sports Experience programs participants receive age-appropriate instruction through organized clinics, skill stations, drills, conditioning, and live game situations led by NYC Parks Staff.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B in Faber Park Recreation Center
address: Multi-Use Room B in Faber Park Recreation Center

name: Yoga
description: This open level class is designed to awaken your mind, body and senses through controlled breathing, simple meditation, and the adoption of specific bodily postures. Please bring a mat and a lock to lock up your bag. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
location_name: Multi-Use Room in St. James Recreation Center
address: Multi-Use Room in St. James Recreation Center

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 5 to 7)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 5 and 7.
address: Gymnasium in Highbridge Recreation Center

name: Hula Hoop Aerobics
description: his creative and fun workout fuses high-energy dance aerobics with hula hoops! Hoops are available for loaning. Participants must bring waist belts (waist trainers). Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. Center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except; towel, water and phone.
location_name: Multi-Use Room B in Pelham Fritz Recreation Center
address: Multi-Use Room B in Pelham Fritz Recreation Center

name: Historic New York: The Little Red Lighthouse
description: The Jeffrey's Hook lighthouse, erected in 1880 and moved to its current site in 1921, has become widely known as the children's literary landmark \"The Little Red Lighthouse and the Great Gray Bridge.\" Rediscover the history of this unique landmark. Open access between 1:00pm and 2:45pm. Participants may need to queue for access due capacity limits inside of the structure. Enter park via overpass closest to Plaza Lafayette. (West 181st Street) and Riverside Drive.
address: Little Red Lighthouse in Fort Washington Park

name: Magnetic Archery and Foam Axe Throwing
description: Our free archery class is a bullseye! Learn the art of archery with free lessons taught by our expert Urban Park Rangers. This program uses safe equipment with magnetic arrows and targets. All equipment will be provided. Foam axe throwing will be available for ages 5 and over.
address: Crotona Nature Center in Crotona Park

name: Park Open Studio
description: Led by a local teaching artist, this drop-in class invites participants to explore creativity through hands-on artmaking under the shade of Scrabble Plaza's trees. Past mediums have included plein-air drawing, printmaking, cyanotypes, and more. All materials are provided by the Washington Square Park Conservancy.
address: Northwest Corner Seating Area in Washington Square Park

name: STRONG Nation®
description: STRONG Nation® combines body weight, muscle conditioning, cardio and plyometric training moves synced to original music that has been specifically designed to match every single move. Every squat, every lunge, every burpee is driven by the music, helping you make it to that last rep, and maybe even five more. Mats are available for those who need one. Participants must bring locks to lock up their bags. The center does not allow anyone to bring their bags to the dance room except: towel, water and phone.
location_name: Dance Room in Constance Baker Motley Recreation Center
address: Dance Room in Constance Baker Motley Recreation Center

name: All That Skitters!
description: How does the Fiddler Crab survive in the salt marsh tides? What other crabs live in the marsh? What happens in the winter? Where do Horseshoe Crabs fit in? Explore the complexities and threats to the fragile salt marsh with Mike Feller, retired Chief Naturalist of the NYC Parks Department. Sponsored by the Salt Marsh Alliance in partnership with the Marine Park Alliance
address: Salt Marsh Nature Center in Marine Park

name: Basketball Clinic at Highbridge Recreation Center (Ages: 8 to 12)
description: This Instructional basketball clinic will provide an opportunity for youth, regardless of skill to have a positive basketball experience followed by league play. Participants will have the opportunity to learn and practice the fundamentals of Basketball and advance their basketball skills through a series of drills. This specific clinic for youth ages 8 and 12.
address: Gymnasium in Highbridge Recreation Center

name: Garden Glow-Up: Plant-Based Beauty Workshop
description: Discover how plants can nourish us inside and out! This hands-on workshop explores simple, plant-based beauty practices using natural ingredients inspired by the garden. Participants will learn how herbs, flowers, and everyday plants can support self-care, wellness, and creativity. All attendees must fill out the Saturdays on the Farm registration and complete the farm waiver: https:\/\/www.harlemgrown.org\/saturdays-on-the-farm
address: Harlem Grown 134th Street Farm

name: Public Gallery Tour
description: Solo and group exhibitions at Glyndor Gallery explore the dynamic relationships between nature, culture, and site. When exhibitions are on view in the spring, summer, and fall, Gallery Greeters lead public exhibition tours on Thursdays and Saturdays at 2PM. Visitors can expect an in-depth look at artworks on view in Glyndor Gallery and in the Sunroom Project Space, showcasing the work of both emerging and established artists in the unique context of a public garden. Each tour lasts approximately 45 minutes. Drop-in event; no registration required. Today's tour features current exhibitions. Questions? Please email us at information@wavehill.org or call 718.549.3200 x251. Glyndor Gallery is wheelchair-accessible. There is an accessible, ground-level entrance at the front of the building with an elevator that provides access to the gallery level. The Sunroom Project Space can be accessed with an ADA-compliant ramp. The restroom on the...
address: Wave Hill

name: Seining
description: Our experienced Urban Park Rangers teach the ecology of our waterways on every seining program. Get ready to get your hands wet; seining programs use nets to catch fish. All equipment is provided; this program is recommended for ages 8 years and older. Participation is first-come, first-served.
location_name: Entrance - East 88th Street and Seaview Avenue
address: Entrance - East 88th Street and Seaview Avenue in Canarsie Park

name: Summer on the Hudson: Story Hour!
description: Weekly story time for kids featuring rotating books and guest readers. Events take place on Saturdays in May, June, August and September. No events May 16 or September 12.
address: 102nd Street Field House in Riverside Park

name: Cocktails on the Farm
description: Enjoy a relaxed evening at Randall's Island Park with Cocktails on our Urban Farm! Sip on farm-fresh cocktails (21+) or complimentary mocktails, while enjoying plant-based bites, crafts, and activities. Afterwards, join us for Movie Night! Bring a blanket and settle in under the stars for an outdoor movie night in the Park. Enjoy sweeping views of the Hell Gate Bridge and the city skyline while watching Zootopia 2, the perfect pick for a fun summer evening! Location: Urban Farm For inquiries about accessibility, to request an accommodation or for event information, please email publicprograms@randallsisland.org.
address: Urban Farm in Randall's Island Park

name: Summer on the Hudson: Silent Disco
description: Grab headphones and party on the Pier at this Quiet Clubbing event featuring three live DJs playing different genres. Arrive early! Line opens at 5:30pm. Headphone supplies are limited and there may be a wait. Rain date September 19.
address: Pier I in Riverside Park South

name: Hudson Classical Theater Company presents: The Dancing Men
description: The Hudson Classical Theater Company Presents The Dancing Men: A Sherlock Holmes Mystery written by Susane Lee, adapted from the short story by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, and directed by Susane Lee and Nicholas Martin-Smith. Hilton Cubitt seeks the help of Sherlock Holmes when he comes across a series of mysterious stick figure drawings resembling \"dancing men\" about his garden. These drawings are linked to his wife, Elsie Patrick, an American woman who harbors a secret past. Holmes needs to decipher these \"dancing men\" before tragedy strikes. The Dancing Men runs July 23rd - August 16th. All shows are Thursdays to Sundays at 6:30pm. No reservations--arrive by 6:15pm for a good seat. Cushions provided. Hudson Classical Theater Company Shows are \"Pay What You Can.\" Audience donations help pay for artists stipends and producti...
address: Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument in Riverside Park

name: RTA Summer Sunset Concert Series featuring The Brooklyners
description: Waterfront sunset concerts sponsored by the Riverside Tennis Association . No performance on July 4th. June 20 - Ron McClure & Friends June 27 - Morrisania Band Project July 11 - The Afro-Latineers July 18 - The Slim Kings July 25 - Richard Baratta August 1 - The Brooklyners August 8 - Durieux The Brooklyners is a NYC reggae band that blends Jamaican standards, French classics, and original conscious tunes into a unique tropical cocktail. The band got its name from its members' diversity and multicultural backgrounds, as well as from i...
address: 96th Street Tennis Courts in Riverside Park

name: Wheel Fun Rentals Celebrates America's 250th with Patriotic LED Bike Ride
description: Wheel Fun Rentals at Boathouse Row is lighting up summer nights in patriotic style as it celebrates America's 250th anniversary with special Red, White & Blue LED Illuminated Surrey Night Rides. Patriotic Surrey Nights Rides are available now and will run through Labor Day Weekend at their Bensonhurst Park and FDR Boardwalk at Sand Lane bike rental locations. For the summer-long celebration, Wheel Fun Rentals has transformed its popular LED-illuminated specialty bikes into dazzling patriotic attractions featuring custom red, white, and blue lighting. Families, couples, and friends can cruise along two of New York City's scenic waterfronts while celebrating America's historic 250th birthday. On July 4th, guests can make the holiday even more memorable by riding to waterfront viewing areas for spectacular fireworks displays. Launching from Bensonhurst Park in Brooklyn, riders can enjoy the festive atmosphere along the waterfront bike path with views across New York Har...

name: Movies Under the Stars: Spy Kids
description: Grab a blanket and come enjoy a movie in a neighborhood park or playground! This summer, NYC Parks and The Mayor's Office of Media and Entertainment will host family-friendly movie showings across the five boroughs. The offspring of former secret agents must come to the rescue when their parents are kidnapped by a madman. Ten years after retiring to raise a family, the couple is lured back into the spy game to thwart a villain plotting world domination. But their plan goes awry and they're captured, leaving it up to their kids to save not only their parents, but all of humanity. Rated PG Parental Guidance Suggested. Some Material May Not Be Suitable For Children. A PG-rated motion picture should be investigated by parents before they let their younger children attend. Movies begin at dusk. This event is FREE and open to the public. Glass bottles and alcoholic beverages are strictly prohibited. In the event of heavy rain or extreme weather, the screenin...
